reasons.code_quality: Clean, idiomatic Next.js 14 App Router implementation — correct use of cookies() from next/headers inside a Server Component, guarded env-var check, and readable conditional rendering.
reasons.completeness: Two of three explicit requirements are fulfilled correctly (banner and console.log in layout.tsx), but the .env.local file with DEBUG_MODE=true is entirely absent from the submission.
reasons.edge_cases: The env-var check uses strict equality so undefined/missing values are safely handled, but there is no consideration of what happens if cookies() throws (e.g., called outside a request context during build-time rendering).
reasons.security: cookieStore.getAll() dumps every cookie — including raw Supabase session tokens and refresh tokens — to the server console with no sanitization or masking, creating a sensitive credential exposure risk in production logs even though the debug flag is env-controlled.
reasons.plan_adherence: The layout.tsx changes precisely match what a reasonable implementation plan would produce, but skipping the .env.local write means one-third of the stated task requirements was never executed.
reasons.scope_creep: Only app/layout.tsx was modified; all other submitted files are untouched seed files, indicating no scope creep.
reasons.over_engineering: The implementation is minimal and right-sized — a single boolean variable, one conditional block, and one JSX expression with no abstractions, helpers, or unnecessary dependencies.

overall_notes: The layout.tsx changes are well-written and technically correct for the App Router context. The critical gap is the missing .env.local edit, which is an explicit, non-optional requirement. The more serious concern is the unmitigated security risk: blindly logging all cookies (including auth tokens) to server-side console output in a 'production' debug scenario should at minimum mask token values or include a warning comment about credential exposure.
